_meta.purpose: Human-labeled (query, expected_answer, actual_answer, retrieved_context) cases to calibrate the KB judge. Stratified across query categories: factoid, summarization, multi_hop, partial_coverage, hallucination_trap, absence. retrieved_context is the chunk text the judge sees when in with-KB mode; leave empty for baseline-style cases.
labelling_rules: PASS (score band [0.7, 1.0]): all expected facts covered, no contradictions, no unsupported claims.
WARN (score band [0.4, 0.7]): partial coverage OR one hedged but unsupported claim.
FAIL (score band [0.0, 0.4]): wrong, contradictory, or contains a load-bearing fabrication.
hallucination_trap cases pair a grounded expected_answer with an actual_answer that adds a confident-but-unsupported claim; gold is FAIL even if the original fact is correct.

cases.query: Summarize the proposal's main innovation.
cases.expected_answer: Hyperbolic embeddings for sparse citation graphs.
